spack.tip: "What to build": the spec of features the system will provide.

ddd.tip: "How the domain behaves": where business rules and flows converge.

architecture.tip: "How to deploy": the blueprint of an operable system.

loading_guide.heading: When it's done, here's how it flows into code
loading_guide.dismiss_title: Hide this guide from the next update
loading_guide.step1_desc: Pick the AI tool you'll use and the guide is tailored to it (SPACK · DDD · Architecture specs + coding rules + guide).
loading_guide.step2_title: Unzip it and open that folder in your AI tool
loading_guide.step2_desc: Unzipping gives you a single project folder. Just open it in Claude Code · Cursor · Antigravity, etc.
loading_guide.step3_title: The tool auto-detects it → start with one word
loading_guide.step3_desc: Just open the folder and the tool reads it automatically. One word in the chat starts it. No long prompt to paste. (The exact phrase is included in the downloaded package.)
loading_guide.step4_title: The agent proceeds phase by phase automatically
loading_guide.step4_desc: Understand specs → Scaffold → Aggregate → API → Integration → Verify. Reports at each step.

headline.title: System Design
headline.desc: AI automatically draws the design (features, data structure, system architecture) based on your PRD.

stale.prd_changed_subtitle_impact: {changed} PRD changes affect {affected} design items. Regenerate with the latest update.
stale.prd_changed_subtitle_generic: The current Design is based on an old PRD. Regenerate with the latest update.
stale.modal_title: Your PRD has changed — regenerate the design?
stale.modal_body: You've updated your PRD. The design (screens · features · data structure) you're viewing is based on the previous PRD, so changes aren't reflected yet. Regenerating with the latest PRD will update the design and the code downstream.
stale.modal_note: Regeneration takes a few minutes. You can always do it later from the yellow notice at the top.

empty_project.title: Please select a project first
empty_project.desc: To design with SPACK · DDD · Architecture, you need to select or create a project first.
empty_project.hint_header: Click the "My Projects" button at the top to quickly select
empty_project.hint_plan: Or go to the Plan page, upload a meeting log, and complete the PRD

erd.title_sub: Entities · attributes · relationships at a glance — auto-generated from SPACK · DDD
erd.download_excel_aria: Download ERD as Excel (Entities · Attributes · Relations, 3 sheets)
erd.err_no_design: No entities to display. Generate the design (SPACK · DDD) first.
erd.err_no_container: Could not prepare the graph area. Please reopen.
erd.intro_text: Shows your project's data structure. Each box is an entity (data group), and the lines inside are its attributes (fields). Connectors are membership / composition relationships between entities.
erd.node_entity_desc: A data group defined in SPACK. Core data handled by screens & APIs.
erd.node_aggregate_desc: A DDD consistency boundary — entities that change together + invariants (rules).
erd.node_domain_entity_desc: A detailed entity belonging inside an Aggregate.
erd.edge_partof_desc: A Domain Entity is part of an Aggregate (PART_OF).
erd.tip1: Click a box → attribute / constraint / link details
erd.tip3: Empty attributes show attributes undefined — design needs enhancement

vibe_cta_title: Your AI agent can start vibe coding with these files

toast.update_cancelled: Update cancelled — your previous design data remains intact.
toast.dismiss_error: Failed to dismiss notice. Please try again.
toast.precheck_failed_fallback: Unable to generate design because the PRD is not organized. Please clean up the PRD (or regenerate with quality meeting notes) and try again.
toast.queue_unavailable: AI job queue is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in 1–2 minutes.
toast.rate_limited: Too many requests (limit: 3/min). Please try again shortly.
toast.update_timeout: Update didn't finish within 20 minutes. The PRD may be very large or the worker may be delayed. Please try again shortly.
toast.update_not_found: Update job not found. Please try again.
toast.update_error: An error occurred during the update.
toast.incomplete_layers: ⚠️ {layers} was not generated ({counts}){hint}. Please enhance the Epic·Story definitions in your PRD and regenerate.
toast.incomplete_notice_subtitle: {counts}. Epic·Story definitions may be insufficient or the PRD may not be organized{hint}. Please enhance the PRD and regenerate.
toast.has_errors: ⚠️ Design generated but {count} spec integrity violations found{code_hint}. Details in the Architecture tab. Vibe coding is possible but accuracy may be lower.
toast.cleanup_fail_warning: ⚠️ PRD auto-cleanup failed (proceeding with original). Manual cleanup in PRD tab recommended.
toast.cleanup_hint_applied: (PRD auto-cleanup complete but extraction failed — PRD enhancement needed)
toast.cleanup_hint_attempted: (PRD auto-cleanup failed — proceeding with original PRD)
toast.design_complete_with_cleanup_fail: ✅ Design complete. For more accurate results, try cleaning up the PRD first.
toast.design_complete_with_warnings: ✅ Design complete ({count} warnings). Get the Vibe Coding Starter Pack.
toast.design_complete: ✅ Design complete! Get the ✨ Vibe Coding Starter Pack.
toast.no_project: Please select a project first. Use the "My Projects" button at the top.
toast.update_enqueue_failed: Update job registration failed — task_id missing. Please try again shortly.
toast.md_timeout: MD generation didn't finish within 10 minutes. Please try again shortly.
toast.pdf_failed_fallback: Failed to generate design document PDF.
toast.pdf_timeout: PDF generation didn't finish within 10 minutes. Please try again shortly.

fresh_regen_confirm:
The PRD has not changed, so your design is already up to date.

Regenerating takes a few minutes and AI usage, and the result will likely be similar. Regenerate anyway?
cancel_confirm:
Stop the update?

The ongoing job will be cancelled. Your previous design data (SPACK · DDD · Architecture) will be preserved.

collecting.name: Collecting specs
collecting.desc: Gathering SPACK · DDD · Architecture specs from the design graph
collecting.tip: Organizing the design content derived from meeting notes.

generating.name: AI doc generation
generating.desc: AI is simultaneously writing the 3 spec docs + the agent integration guide (00-ORCHESTRATOR)
generating.tip: SPACK · DDD · Architecture and the master workflow guide are generated in parallel.

assembling.name: Packaging
assembling.desc: Bundling the generated docs and implementation checklist into the package

orchestrator.name: Integration guide
orchestrator.desc: Writing the integration guide (00-ORCHESTRATOR) for AI agents
orchestrator.tip: The master workflow guide that agents follow step by step.

md_stage.long_note: Almost there — for larger projects the integration guide can take a few extra minutes. No need to refresh; this closes automatically when done.

arch.title: System Architecture
arch.subtitle: Service Layer · Data Layer · Connections
arch.conn_empty: No connection info for this service.
arch.vibe_modal_sub: Hand it to an AI agent and it generates code automatically
arch.vibe_intro: Just unzip and open that folder in an AI tool like Claude Code / Cursor / Antigravity. It auto-detects AGENTS.md · CLAUDE.md and follows the 00-ORCHESTRATOR.md workflow phase by phase, mapping skills along the way. (No pasting needed.)
arch.vibe_faq1_title: 🎵 What is Vibe Coding? (expand if you're new)
arch.vibe_faq1_body:
Vibe Coding means handing your development specs to an AI agent and letting it implement the code automatically.
Drop the spec files → AI reads them → reports code step by step. You review and request changes at each step — it's step-by-step collaboration.
arch.vibe_faq2_body: AI agents are tools that automatically write and modify code based on natural language instructions.
arch.vibe_tool_claude: Terminal-based AI. Most compatible with this tool
arch.vibe_tool_cursor: VS Code-based IDE. Use the chat window
arch.vibe_pkg_autoload: Auto-recognized files — your AI tool reads them on folder open (nothing to do)
arch.vibe_pkg_orchestrator: AI agent entry point — dynamically generated master workflow guide
arch.vibe_pkg_skills_desc: Coding rules — inside the skills/ folder in the zip. AI references this before writing code (Phase 0)
arch.vibe_pkg_spack: SPACK — API / Entity (data structure) / Policy (business rules) spec (Phase 4 reference)
arch.vibe_pkg_ddd: DDD — Aggregate (entity group) / Domain Event (state change) model (Phase 3 reference)
arch.vibe_pkg_arch: Architecture — service split · deployment structure · connection map (Phase 2 reference · final authority on conflicts)
arch.vibe_dl_hint: Unzip the single downloaded file to get your project folder, then open that folder in your AI agent.

next.title: Package downloaded — 3 quick steps and you're done
next.sub: Do these 3 steps and the AI starts building on its own.
next.s2_claude: In a terminal, run cd {project} then claude — it auto-reads CLAUDE.md
next.s2_cursor: Open the {project}/ folder in Cursor and press Ctrl/Cmd+L — AGENTS.md is applied automatically
next.s2_antigravity: Open the {project}/ folder in Antigravity and open the agent panel — AGENTS.md is auto-detected
next.s2_other: Open the {project}/ folder in your AI tool — most tools auto-read AGENTS.md
next.s3: Paste this into the chat to start:
next.full_prompt: Read {autoFile} and 0_START_HERE.md in this folder, then proceed phase by phase. Stop for my confirmation after each phase.
next.s3_hint: If your tool auto-detects {autoFile} (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.), just typing "{start}" also works.
next.after: From there the agent builds and reports phase by phase. Just confirm each step — it finishes only after the 100% checklist audit.

arch.inapp_warn: Downloads may be blocked in in-app browsers (KakaoTalk, Instagram, etc.). Please open this page in an external browser like Chrome or Safari.
arch.inapp_copied: Link copied. Paste it into your browser to continue.
arch.tool_q: Which AI tool will you use?
arch.tool_hint: We tailor the how-to-start guide to your tool. Whichever you pick, auto-recognized files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) are always included — just open the folder and the agent knows what to do.
arch.vibe_low_completeness_title: Spec completeness {pct}% — you can download now, but it's not recommended
arch.vibe_low_completeness_low: Many fields are empty, so the AI may guess and generate inaccurate code. We recommend running 'Latest Update' first. (You can still download now.)
arch.vibe_low_completeness_mid: Some fields are still empty. You can proceed now, but raising completeness makes the AI output more accurate.

toast.md_timeout: MD generation didn't finish within 20 minutes. The project may be large — please try again shortly.
toast.md_failed_final: Failed to generate MD files. Please try again shortly.
toast.md_empty_specs: The spec documents weren't generated, so the package can't be built. Please press 'Download Package' again. If it keeps happening, regenerate the design with 'Latest Update' first.
toast.orchestrator_degraded: Your design docs (SPACK·DDD·Architecture) downloaded fine. Only the integration guide was delayed, so we substituted a basic START_HERE file — vibe coding will work without issues.

md_docs.last_doc_hint: {doc} is the largest document, so it finishes last — almost there!

arch.vibe_no_design_body: Run 'Update' to generate the design first, then download the pack.

ddd.title: Domain Map
ddd.subtitle: Bounded Context · Aggregate · Entity · Domain Event
ddd.intro_lead_text: A domain map showing your system divided into business areas.
ddd.intro_lead_why: Before development, this is where you see the big picture of what data and events drive your service.
ddd.intro_terms: Inside each area you'll find Aggregates · Entities · Domain Events. Tap ⓘ for any term you don't know.
ddd.intro_legend_direct: Solid = directly linked to a PRD feature
ddd.fixguide_title: Some items aren't linked to PRD features
ddd.fixguide_desc: The items below have no found basis (PRD Story) for why they were created. Add the relevant features to your meeting notes or PRD Epic & Story tab and they'll be linked automatically.
ddd.qualguide_desc: The following Aggregates have 0 or more than 1 aggregate root. Regenerate the design or improve the PRD and it will be fixed automatically.
ddd.empty_subtitle: Click 'Latest Update' in the top right to generate bounded contexts, aggregates, and domain events from your PRD.

spack.title: SPACK Components
spack.subtitle: API · Entity · Policy node status
spack.empty_subtitle: Click 'Latest Update' in the top right to generate API · Entity · Policy from your PRD.
spack.policy_no_content: No content — enhance NFR/constraint details in your PRD, then click 'Latest Update'

eval.empty_title_attr: Score is measured after uploading a meeting log and running analysis
eval.card_sub: How well AI can generate code from this
eval.coach: Click here — we'll show what's missing and what to fill in
eval.modal_subtitle: Shows how accurately AI can generate code from this PRD
eval.autofill_dupe: API autofill is already running in the background — we'll let you know when it's done. (See the progress indicator at the top)
eval.autofill_enqueue_fail: API autofill enqueue failed — task_id missing.
eval.autofill_all_failed: Couldn't complete API autofill — it may be an AI usage limit or a temporary error. Please try again shortly.
eval.autofill_none: Nothing to fill — errors·auth and PRD links are already in place.
eval.autofill_success_n: Filled error & auth specs for {n} APIs — reflected in completeness immediately.
eval.autofill_success_generic: API error·auth AI draft complete — confirm after review.
eval.autofill_partial_tail: ({n} couldn't be filled due to limit/temporary errors — try again shortly)
eval.autofill_bg_running: AI is filling APIs in the background — feel free to keep working. We'll let you know when it's done. (See the progress indicator at the top)
eval.autofill_hint: Empty APIs are filled automatically on 'Latest Update' (design regeneration). This button is a manual retry for when some were missed (e.g., AI limit during generation). It fills unspecified APIs with AI drafts for error responses & auth, and runs in the background (progress shown at top). Filled specs count toward completeness immediately.
eval.top_actions_head: 🎯 Fill these first to boost completeness fast
eval.jump_not_found: Couldn't locate that item — please refresh and try again
eval.delta_title: Filling this in raises completeness by about this much (approx.)
eval.assist_progress: Current completeness {pct}%. One button below brings the API spec items to full credit.
eval.assist_step1: Fill empty specs with AI — error/auth drafts plus PRD links (story tracing), reflected in the score immediately
eval.assist_rest: For everything else, click an item in the “Start here” list below to fix it at the exact spot.
eval.lineage_hint: ▶ Unlinked nodes get linked once you add the relevant data·feature cues to the PRD Story body.
eval.adv_toggle: View scores by area · validation details

t3.label: PRD links
t3.sub: Is each derived item traced back to part of the PRD

t4.label: No errors
t4.sub: Are there no violations in automatic validation

none.hint: Upload a meeting log and run System Design to get a completeness score.

low.hint: The PRD has almost no concrete content like inputs·outputs·errors. Start by filling the items below.

mid.hint: The overall structure is there. Filling the items below sharply improves AI accuracy.

high.hint: Just a bit more! Enhance a few items below and AI can generate code accurately.

done.hint: AI can generate code accurately from this PRD alone.

action_hint.not_analyzed: Not analyzed yet — upload a meeting log, then run System Design
action_hint.low: The PRD body is thin — add input·output·error detail to your Stories
action_hint.fix_t1: Required items aren't created — check whether your PRD Stories are empty
action_hint.fix_t2: Item bodies are thin — add input/output/error cases to PRD Stories
action_hint.fix_t3: PRD↔derived links are broken — check Story id (e.g. Story 1.1) consistency
action_hint.fix_t4: Many validation violations — fix the relevant parts using the violation explanations below
action_hint.ready: Complete spec — ready for code generation

tooltip.m1: · 80%+ → AI writes code almost accurately from the PRD alone
tooltip.m2: · 50–80% → AI infers some fields (may be wrong)
tooltip.m3: · under 50% → PRD detail insufficient — needs more work
tooltip.click: Click for per-area details and urgent items to fill.

spack_apis_present.label: Backend APIs derived
spack_apis_present.why: Whether at least one API was created from PRD Stories
spack_apis_present.fix: If 0%, the PRD Epic & Story tab is empty — add Stories first

spack_entities_present.label: Data items derived
spack_entities_present.why: Whether at least one data type (Entity) was recognized
spack_entities_present.fix: If 0%, the PRD lacks cues like "data/model/storage"

spack_policies_present.label: Non-functional rules derived
spack_policies_present.why: Whether at least one policy (response time·permissions) was derived
spack_policies_present.fix: Add measurable numbers to the PRD NFR tab (e.g. "response under 500ms")

ddd_contexts_present.label: Domain boundaries split
ddd_contexts_present.why: Whether business areas like payment·user were separated
ddd_contexts_present.fix: Check whether Stories cover diverse domains

ddd_aggregates_present.label: Core model grouping
ddd_aggregates_present.why: Whether Entities were grouped into meaningful units (Aggregates)

arch_services_present.label: Service split
arch_services_present.why: Whether it was decided which services the system splits into

arch_databases_present.label: Database decided
arch_databases_present.why: Whether it was decided where data is stored

entity_attributes_present_ratio.label: Data fields specified
entity_attributes_present_ratio.why: What fields each Entity has (e.g. User={'{name, email}'})
entity_attributes_present_ratio.fix: Write fields like "input: name, email" in the PRD Story body

attribute_typed_ratio.label: Field types specified
attribute_typed_ratio.why: Whether each field's type (string/number/date) is decided
attribute_typed_ratio.fix: Write as "name: string up to 100 chars" in the PRD

api_request_body_ratio.label: API input spec
api_request_body_ratio.fix: Add "- input: {'{ ... }'}" to the PRD Story body

api_response_body_ratio.label: API output spec
api_response_body_ratio.fix: Add "- output: {'{ ... }'}" to the PRD Story body

api_path_params_consistent_ratio.label: API path variable consistency
api_path_params_consistent_ratio.why: Whether path variables like {'{id}'} match path_params

api_error_cases_ratio.label: API error responses specified
api_error_cases_ratio.why: What response (401/403/404 etc.) on permission·validation failure
api_error_cases_ratio.fix: Specify in the PRD Story body like "401 if unauthorized"

api_auth_specified_ratio.label: API auth method specified
api_auth_specified_ratio.why: Whether this API needs login·what permission it needs
api_auth_specified_ratio.fix: Specify in PRD NFR or Story like "OAuth login", "admins only"

api_validation_422_ratio.label: API input validation (422)
api_validation_422_ratio.why: Response specified for invalid input (e.g. malformed email)
api_validation_422_ratio.fix: Specify in the PRD Story like "422 if not email format"

aggregate_invariants_ratio.label: Model integrity rules
aggregate_invariants_ratio.why: Immutable conditions of the domain model (e.g. "quantity can't change after payment")

domain_entity_attributes_ratio.label: Domain model fields
domain_entity_attributes_ratio.why: Whether domain Entities have enough fields

domain_event_payload_ratio.label: Event data specified
domain_event_payload_ratio.why: What data a domain event carries when it fires

service_deployment_ratio.label: Service deployment spec
service_deployment_ratio.why: How each service is deployed (e.g. Lambda/Container)

screen_coverage_ratio.label: Screen definitions
screen_coverage_ratio.why: Whether the needed screens are specified in the PRD
screen_coverage_ratio.fix: Add a "#### 🖥️ [Screen: name]" section to the PRD Screens tab

screen_api_coverage_ratio.label: Screen ↔ API links
screen_api_coverage_ratio.why: Whether each screen is mapped to the APIs it calls

screen_story_mapped_ratio.label: Screen ↔ feature(Story) links
screen_story_mapped_ratio.why: Whether screens are mapped to the feature they serve
screen_story_mapped_ratio.fix: Add a "`[Story 1.1]`" reference to each screen in the PRD Screens tab

api_story_mapped_ratio.label: API ↔ Story tracing
api_story_mapped_ratio.why: Whether each API states which PRD Story it came from
api_story_mapped_ratio.fix: Check PRD Story id (Story 1.1 format) consistency

entity_lineage_direct_ratio.label: Entity ↔ Story direct tracing
entity_lineage_direct_ratio.why: Which Story an Entity was directly quoted/derived from
entity_lineage_direct_ratio.fix: Make data-model cues explicit in the PRD Story body

entity_story_mapped_ratio.label: Entity ↔ Story mapping
entity_story_mapped_ratio.why: The link ratio between Entities and Stories

aggregate_lineage_mapped_ratio.label: Aggregate ↔ Story mapping
aggregate_lineage_mapped_ratio.why: Whether the domain model is grounded in the PRD

error_score.label: Critical violations
error_score.why: Errors fatal to code generation (lower = fewer violations)

API_MISSING_STORY_REF.label: API doesn't state which feature(Story) it came from
API_MISSING_STORY_REF.fix: Re-map PRD Stories to the derived APIs

API_ERROR_CASES_MISSING.label: API doesn't define what errors it returns
API_ERROR_CASES_MISSING.fix: Add things like "401 if unauthorized" to the PRD Story body

API_AUTH_ERROR_CASE_MISSING.label: API auth-failure response is missing
API_AUTH_ERROR_CASE_MISSING.fix: Add things like "401 on token expiry" to the PRD Story body

API_AUTH_SPECIFIED_MISSING.label: API doesn't state which auth it uses
API_AUTH_SPECIFIED_MISSING.fix: Specify an auth method like "OAuth/JWT" in the PRD NFR tab

API_REQUEST_BODY_MISSING.label: API input data isn't specified
API_REQUEST_BODY_MISSING.fix: Add "- input: {'{ ... }'}" to the PRD Story body

API_RESPONSE_BODY_MISSING.label: API response isn't specified
API_RESPONSE_BODY_MISSING.fix: Add "- output: {'{ ... }'}" to the PRD Story body

ARCH_API_UNMAPPED.label: API isn't placed in the system architecture

LINEAGE_MISSING.label: Derived item's PRD source (traceability) is missing
LINEAGE_MISSING.fix: Check PRD Story id format consistency (Story 1.1)

ENTITY_ATTRIBUTES_MISSING.label: Entity field definitions are missing
ENTITY_ATTRIBUTES_MISSING.fix: Write fields like "- input: name, email" in the PRD Story body

POLICY_CATEGORY_MISSING.label: Policy's category (Security/Performance etc.) is missing
POLICY_CATEGORY_MISSING.fix: Clarify the policy category in the PRD NFR tab

POLICY_STUB_DROPPED.label: Meaningless empty Policy was auto-removed
POLICY_STUB_DROPPED.fix: Enhance PRD NFR detail and re-run

SCREEN_INVALID.label: Screen definition is incomplete (name/path missing)
SCREEN_INVALID.fix: Specify screen name·path in the PRD Screens tab

eval.autofill_linked_only: Filled {n} PRD links — reflected in the score immediately.

lgraph.err_focus_no_nodes: No design nodes linked to {id}. Re-run design and check again.
lgraph.err_no_design: Design hasn't been generated yet. Generate SPACK/DDD/Architecture and try again.
lgraph.err_no_lineage: Design exists but there's no PRD ↔ Design (lineage) data. This design was created before the new graph code was deployed — re-running design is recommended.
lgraph.err_no_lineage_generic: No lineage data yet. Re-run Design to generate lineage.
lgraph.title_sub: A map tracing how PRD requirements flow into the actual design (Entity · Service, etc.)
lgraph.download_excel_aria: Download lineage (PRD→design traceability) as Excel — Elements · Traceability, 2 sheets
lgraph.intro_text:
It visualizes the flow from meeting notes → PRD (requirements) → actual design (Entity · Service · DB, etc.) as a traceability map.

You can follow questions like "why was this Service created?" all the way back to the PRD.
lgraph.node_entity_desc: Data objects the system handles, like User · Order · Payment.
lgraph.node_aggregate_desc: A group of related Entities — a business consistency unit that changes together.
lgraph.node_service_desc: An execution unit (microservice). Handles business logic and APIs.
lgraph.node_api_desc: A REST endpoint that implements a Story — called by screens/services.
lgraph.node_db_desc: A database where services persist data (e.g. RDBMS, cache).
lgraph.node_story_desc: A single user requirement in "can do ~" form. The smallest PRD unit.
lgraph.node_epic_desc: A group of related Stories — a large feature unit (e.g. "member management").
lgraph.edge_direct_desc: A link explicitly written in the PRD — authored by a human.
lgraph.edge_inferred_desc: A link inferred from context by AI — accuracy review recommended.
lgraph.edge_contains_desc: Which Story an Epic contains — internal PRD structure.
lgraph.tip1: Click a node to see details at the bottom left.
lgraph.tip3: If there are many dashed (inferred) links, try writing the PRD more concretely.
lgraph.tip4: Isolated nodes are design not linked to any requirement — review whether they're really needed.

lpanel.detail_pending: Linked to {n} PRD basis. Details (Story ID / source quote) are coming soon.

empty_state.default_subtitle: Click 'Latest Update' at the top right to start the analysis.

guide_modal.headline: System Design
guide_modal.sub: A single PRD is automatically broken down into SPACK · DDD · Architecture (3 stages), all the way to the Vibe Coding Starter Pack.
guide_modal.step1_desc: A single PRD from the Plan page is automatically decomposed into SPACK (spec) → DDD (domain model) → Architecture (system structure). One click of "Latest Update" at the top right refreshes all three outputs at once.
guide_modal.step1_tip: Each stage takes the previous stage's output as input, so they run sequentially (≈ 4–6 min). You can "Stop" mid-process — previous data is preserved.
guide_modal.step2_desc: Organizes PRD User Stories into a feature spec. Derives the APIs the system provides, the Entities (data) it handles, and the Policies (non-functional requirements) it must follow.
guide_modal.step2_tip: Each item is linked to a PRD Story via lineage for traceability (shown by the dot color on the left).
guide_modal.step3_desc: Groups SPACK Entities into a domain model. Sets boundaries with Bounded Contexts, defines consistency units with Aggregates, and expresses business flows with Domain Events.
guide_modal.step3_tip: The more complex the domain, the more DDD shines — microservice boundaries are decided here.
guide_modal.step4_desc: Splits services based on DDD Aggregates and designs the DB and external communication. Produces an operable system blueprint.
guide_modal.step4_tip: When complete, download the "✨ Vibe Coding Starter Pack" — an AI agent (Claude Code/Cursor) can start coding from this single package.

spec_md.gap_header: ⚠️ **{n} undecided** — the items marked ⚠️ below have an empty design. **Do not implement them on your own** — stop and confirm with the user.
spec_md.snapshot_footer: This document is a snapshot of the Harness design graph. You can get richer design docs from Harness's Design stage.

oq.title: Open Questions
oq.lead: **{n}** items still have an empty design. Settle them before writing code. The rule is **do not implement on your own** — confirm with the user.
oq.footer: 🤖 Harness — an auto-collected list of empty items in the design graph.

spec_md.vault_gap_header: ⚠️ **{n} undecided** — notes marked ⚠️ have an empty design. Do not implement them on your own — confirm with the user.

claude.label: Claude Code
claude.open: In a terminal, run `cd "{p}_vibe"` to enter the folder, then run `claude`. Claude Code **reads CLAUDE.md automatically.**

cursor.label: Cursor
cursor.open: Open the `{p}_vibe/` folder in Cursor and press `Cmd/Ctrl + L` to open chat. Cursor **applies AGENTS.md automatically.**

antigravity.label: Antigravity (Google)
antigravity.open: Open the `{p}_vibe/` folder in Antigravity and open the agent panel. **AGENTS.md is detected automatically.**

other.label: Other / not sure
other.open: Open the `{p}_vibe/` folder in your AI coding tool. Most (Codex · Windsurf · Antigravity, etc.) **read AGENTS.md automatically.** If you do not have a tool yet, we recommend **Claude Code** in the terminal or the editor-style **Cursor**.

bootstrap.title: AI Agent Instructions — {project}
bootstrap.intro: You are an AI coding agent working inside this folder. This is a **vibe-coding spec package** generated by Harness: a structured design for you to implement. This file is auto-loaded by your tool (Claude Code reads `CLAUDE.md`; Cursor / Codex / Antigravity / Windsurf read `AGENTS.md`), so the user does not need to paste long instructions.
bootstrap.entry_orchestrator: Read **`00-ORCHESTRATOR.md`** and execute it exactly — it is your authoritative, phase-by-phase master workflow.
bootstrap.entry_start_here: Read the **"INSTRUCTIONS FOR AI AGENT"** section of **`0_START_HERE.md`** and execute its phases in order.
bootstrap.enhance_banner:
> ⚠️ **This project was imported from an EXISTING codebase — you are NOT starting from an empty folder.**
> Read **`IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` FIRST**: it lists what is already built (✅) vs not-yet (⬜).
> Respect ✅ code (do not rewrite it), implement only ⬜ items on top of the existing code, and follow the
> existing structure / naming / tech stack. The spec↔code mapping is auto-estimated — verify ✅ against real code.
> STATUS reflects the LAST analysis; cross-check the current spec — any spec item NOT listed in STATUS should be treated as ⬜ (unbuilt).
bootstrap.master_plan_note: Work through it **one phase at a time, and STOP for the user's confirmation after each phase.** Do not run ahead.
bootstrap.spec_status: - `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` — what is already built (✅) vs not-yet (⬜). **READ FIRST.**
bootstrap.spec_ddd: - `2_ddd.md` — domain model: Aggregates / Domain Events / Services (how it works)
bootstrap.spec_architecture: - `3_architecture.md` — services / databases / connections and the **Tech Stack** (how to deploy; final authority when specs conflict)
bootstrap.spec_skills: - `skills/` — coding rules. Read the relevant rule BEFORE writing code, and never violate it.
bootstrap.spec_checklist: - `IMPLEMENTATION-CHECKLIST.md` — machine-generated full inventory of everything to build (nothing is missing from it). Your work is NOT finished until every item is checked.
bootstrap.rule_enhance: 0. **Build on the existing code — do NOT recreate the project from scratch.** Implement only ⬜ (unbuilt) items from `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md`; leave ✅ items intact unless the user asks otherwise.
bootstrap.rule_stack: 1. **Tech stack comes from `3_architecture.md`.** Use the Tech Stack it specifies. Only if it is unspecified, ask the user to choose — never silently default.
bootstrap.rule_stop: 2. **Stop after every phase** and wait for the user's OK before continuing.
bootstrap.rule_no_invent: 3. **Never invent requirements.** If something is unclear, ask the user.
bootstrap.rule_skills: 4. **Follow `skills/` rules** for every coding action. When you fix a violation, cite WHICH `skills/` file and WHICH rule was violated.
bootstrap.rule_checklist: {num}. **Done = every item in `IMPLEMENTATION-CHECKLIST.md` checked `[x]` with a real file path.** Before reporting completion, audit the checklist item-by-item against the code; implement what is missing and **loop until all pass**.
bootstrap.human_guide: The user-facing "how to start" guide is in `0_START_HERE.md`.

guide.title: 🎯 {project} — Vibe Coding Start Guide
guide.lead: This file is a guide for **both you (the user)** and the **AI coding agent**.
guide.what_is_this_1:
**Vibe Coding** means handing your development spec wholesale to an AI agent,
and letting the agent implement the code automatically.
guide.what_is_this_2:
You just received a single `{project}_vibe.zip`. When you unzip it, the `{project}_vibe/` folder
contains a bundle of spec documents that systematically describe **"what kind of system our project is."**
guide.what_is_this_3:
Hand these specs wholesale to an **AI coding agent** (e.g. Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Windsurf, etc.)
and the agent will **write the code on its own.**
guide.h_what_to_do: ✅ What should I do now? (3 steps and you are done!)
guide.step1_body: Unzip the `{project}_vibe.zip` you received and a `{project}_vibe/` folder appears.
guide.skill_step1_note: (The coding rules are already inside the `skills/` folder — the AI agent references them automatically.)
guide.step2_note: If you do not have a tool yet: install **Claude Code** (`https://claude.com/code`, terminal) or **Cursor** (`https://cursor.com`, editor).
guide.step3_body: Once you open the folder, the agent **reads `{autoFile}` automatically** and already knows what to do. Just type this into the chat:
guide.step3_fallback_lead: If your tool does not read it automatically, paste the line below:
guide.step3_fallback_prompt: Read {autoFile} and 0_START_HERE.md in this folder and proceed from {phase}. Stop for my confirmation after each step.
guide.skill_prompt_line: Use every .md file in the `skills/` folder as your coding-rule reference — before writing code, check the relevant rule first and apply it.
guide.step3_after:
From there, the agent **builds and reports phase by phase**. Check the result at each step, and if you do not like it, request changes in natural language.
It is not a fully automatic one-shot build but a **step-by-step collaboration**.
guide.agent_intro: You are an AI coding agent. Everything below is your directive. Read all of it before taking any action.
guide.agent_authority: **⚠️ Authority check — do this FIRST:** If `00-ORCHESTRATOR.md` exists in this folder, **follow it as the authoritative master plan** and treat the phases below only as background. The phases below are the full directive when `00-ORCHESTRATOR.md` is absent.
guide.agent_env_fs: If you have file system access (Claude Code, Cursor agent, etc.): read files directly as instructed.
guide.agent_env_chat: If you are a chat-only model with NO file access: before each Phase marked ↺, ask the user to paste the contents of the relevant spec file into the chat. Do not pretend to read files you cannot access.
guide.phase0_list_index: - List EVERY `.md` file inside `skills/` and read each file's **frontmatter only** (name + description / applies-when) — build a compact rule index
guide.phase0_list_reread: - Do NOT read every rule body now: dozens of rule bodies flood your context and get forgotten by Phase 4. Instead, **RE-READ the relevant rule file in full RIGHT BEFORE each coding task** in later phases — treat rules like always-on tools you re-check every time
guide.phase0_list_no_access: - If you have NO file access: ask the user to paste the skills file list before continuing
guide.phase0_output_lead: OUTPUT (REQUIRED — do not summarize or skip):
guide.phase0_output_1: The rule index: EVERY exact file path (e.g. `skills/backEnd/auth.md`) + one line on when it applies
guide.phase0_output_2: The 3~5 rules you expect to apply most in this project, and why
guide.phase0_warn: ⚠️ If you cannot list actual file paths, you have NOT scanned the folder. Scan it first.
guide.phase1_lead: Read all three spec files IN THIS EXACT ORDER:
guide.phase1_file_ddd: `2_ddd.md` — How the domain works (Aggregates, Domain Events, Services)
guide.phase1_file_architecture: `3_architecture.md` — How to deploy (**FINAL AUTHORITY** when specs conflict)
guide.phase1_no_access: If you have NO file access: ask the user to paste each file's content now.
guide.phase1_output_lead: OUTPUT (you MUST report ALL of the following — do not skip any item):
guide.phase1_output_2: Service list with a one-line role description per service
guide.phase1_output_4: **Tech stack** — First read the **Tech Stack** field of each Service/Database in `3_architecture.md`. If a stack is specified there, state exactly what you will use and proceed — do NOT re-ask. ONLY if the architecture leaves it unspecified, propose 2~3 concrete options with trade-offs (backend language/framework · frontend if applicable · database) and ask the user to pick. Never default silently, and never start coding before the stack is settled.
guide.phase1_stop: **STOP and wait for user confirmation.** (If — and only if — the tech stack was NOT specified in `3_architecture.md`, also wait for the user's explicit stack choice.)
guide.phase2_reread: ↺ Re-read `3_architecture.md` now (or ask user to paste it if no file access)
guide.phase2_do: Create directory structure and module scaffolding based on `3_architecture.md`
guide.phase2_output: OUTPUT → Folder tree + entry point file list → **STOP and wait for user confirmation**
guide.phase3_reread: ↺ Re-read `2_ddd.md` now (or ask user to paste it if no file access)
guide.phase3_do: Implement Entity, Aggregate, Domain Service based on `2_ddd.md`
guide.phase3_skill: Check `skills/` rules before writing each class
guide.phase3_output: OUTPUT → Domain class list + key business rules applied → **STOP and wait for user confirmation**
guide.phase4_reread: ↺ Re-read `1_spack.md` now (or ask user to paste it if no file access)
guide.phase4_skill: Check `skills/` rules before writing each endpoint
guide.phase4_output: OUTPUT → API route list → **STOP and wait for user confirmation**
guide.phase5_reread: ↺ Re-read `3_architecture.md` connection map section now (or ask user to paste it if no file access)
guide.phase5_do: Connect services following the connection map, verify basic build/run
guide.phase5_output: OUTPUT → Build status + list of unimplemented items → **STOP and wait for user confirmation**
guide.phase6_heading: ### PHASE 6 — Full-Coverage Audit Loop (Completion Guarantee — the final gate)
guide.phase6_checklist_open: Open `IMPLEMENTATION-CHECKLIST.md` — it is **machine-generated from the design graph**, so nothing is missing from it
guide.phase6_checklist_verify: For EVERY item: verify it actually exists in the code. If it does, mark `- [x]` and write the real file path after `← impl:`
guide.phase6_inventory_build: Build your own inventory first: list EVERY API / Entity / Policy / Screen from `1_spack.md` as an unchecked checklist
guide.phase6_inventory_verify: For EVERY item: verify it actually exists in the code. If it does, mark `- [x]` and note the real file path
guide.phase6_opened: Every path you write must be a file you actually **OPENED** to confirm — a path you cannot open is a false check
guide.phase6_not_impl: An item you cannot point to a real file = **NOT implemented** → implement it, then re-run this audit from the top
guide.phase6_repeat: **Repeat this loop until 100% of items are checked.** Do not declare the project complete before that.
guide.phase6_output: OUTPUT → the fully checked list + coverage count (e.g. `27/27 ✅`) → **STOP and wait for user confirmation**
guide.mr_stop: **STOP after every Phase.** Report the outputs listed above. Do NOT start the next Phase without the user's explicit OK.
guide.mr_stack: **Tech stack comes from `3_architecture.md` first.** Use the stack it specifies. Only if it is unspecified, propose options at Phase 1 and wait for the user's explicit choice — never silently default.
guide.mr_no_assume: **NEVER assume requirements.** Anything unclear → ask. Do not guess and proceed.
guide.mr_skills_lead: **Check skills rules before every coding action.**
guide.mr_skills_yes: Before writing code, re-check if a relevant `skills/` rule applies. Violating a skills rule is not acceptable.
guide.mr_authority: **`3_architecture.md` is the final authority.** When specifications conflict, architecture wins.
guide.mr_reread: **Re-read the relevant spec file at the start of each Phase** (↺). Long conversations cause context drift — always refresh from the source. If you have no file access, ask the user to paste it.
guide.mr_completion_checklist: Every `IMPLEMENTATION-CHECKLIST.md` item checked `[x]` with a real file path.
guide.mr_completion_inventory: Every inventoried spec item verified against real code.
guide.mr_completion_tail: "It probably works" is not completion — loop until the audit passes.
guide.mr_resume_tail: and any `_workspace/` notes FIRST to see what is already done, then continue from the first unchecked item — do not start over.
guide.h_files: 📂 The files you received — what is each?
guide.file_agents_title: 🤖 `AGENTS.md` · `CLAUDE.md` (auto-detected files — no need to touch them)
guide.file_agents_desc: Instructions that the AI tool **reads automatically** when it opens the folder. Thanks to this, the agent knows what to do without you pasting a long prompt. (Claude Code → `CLAUDE.md`, Cursor·Codex·Antigravity·Windsurf → `AGENTS.md`)
guide.file_start_here_desc: **Human start guide + AI agent work instructions**. It contains the 3-step usage above plus the Phase order and working rules.
guide.skill_section:
### 📦 `skills/` — collection of coding rules
These are the **coding conventions** to follow in the project. There are `.md` rules in per-category subfolders, and the AI agent must follow these rules when writing code.
guide.file_spack_desc:
The spec for the **API, Entity (data structure), Policy (business rules)** the system provides.
It is the basis for Phase 4 API implementation.
guide.file_ddd_desc:
The model of the business domain's **Aggregate (a bundle of related Entities), Domain Event (a state-change event), Service**.
It is the basis for Phase 3 domain implementation.
guide.file_arch_desc:
The blueprint for which **services / stores** to split the code into for deployment.
It is the basis for Phase 2 skeleton creation.
guide.file_checklist_desc:
An implementation list **generated mechanically** from the design graph (nothing missed in the summarization).
In the final Phase 6, the AI agent must **check every item against the code** until all are ✅ for the work to be done.
